attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

20	# GitHub Actions sets GITHUB_ACTIONS=true. In CI the durability/recovery invariant
21	# MUST be exercised, not silently skipped — a plain skipif on a DB-less runner left
22	# the cross-boundary recovery contract unverified exactly where regressions land
23	# (#260). Locally (no CI), missing Postgres still skips so contributors without a
24	# database can run the rest of the suite.
25	_IN_CI = os.environ.get("GITHUB_ACTIONS", "").lower() == "true"
26	
27	
28	def test_memory_checkpointer():
29	    cp = get_checkpointer(use_memory=True)
30	    assert isinstance(cp, MemorySaver)
31	
32	
33	def _postgres_reachable() -> bool:
34	    try:
35	        import psycopg
36	        with psycopg.connect(get_postgres_connection_string(), connect_timeout=2):
37	            return True
38	    except Exception:
39	        return False
40	
41	
42	def _require_postgres() -> None:
43	    """Skip locally when Postgres is absent, but FAIL in CI — the durability
44	    invariant must never silently skip on the runner where regressions slip in.
45	
46	    Evaluated at call time (in the test body), not at import time like a skipif
47	    decorator, so a service container that finishes coming up just after collection
48	    is still picked up (#260)."""
49	    if _postgres_reachable():
50	        return
51	    if _IN_CI:
52	        pytest.fail(
53	            "Postgres is unreachable in CI — the checkpointer durability/recovery "
54	            "tests must run here (provision the service container and set "
55	            "HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL). The cross-boundary recovery invariant must not "
56	            "silently skip in CI (#260)."
57	        )
58	    pytest.skip("Postgres not reachable (local run without a database)")
59	
60	
61	def test_postgres_checkpointer_round_trips():
62	    _require_postgres()
63	    from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
64	
65	    cp = get_checkpointer(use_memory=False)
66	    assert isinstance(cp, PostgresSaver)
67	    cfg = {"configurable": {"thread_id": "test-cp-roundtrip", "checkpoint_ns": ""}}
68	    # list() must succeed against the pooled connection (no error == healthy pool).
69	    assert isinstance(list(cp.list(cfg)), list)
70	
71	
72	def test_checkpoint_recovers_across_a_fresh_checkpointer():
73	    # Durability across a process/connection boundary (#224): write a checkpoint
74	    # with one checkpointer/pool, then read it back through a SEPARATE checkpointer
75	    # (new pool) — simulating a process restart resuming a parked run.
76	    _require_postgres()
77	    from src.graph import build_graph
78	
79	    run_id = "test-cp-recovery"
80	    cfg = {"configurable": {"thread_id": run_id}}
81	
82	    cp_write = get_checkpointer(use_memory=False)
83	    build_graph(checkpointer=cp_write).invoke(initial_state(run_id=run_id), config=cfg)
84	
85	    cp_read = get_checkpointer(use_memory=False)  # fresh pool / different connections
86	    recovered = build_graph(checkpointer=cp_read).get_state(cfg)
87	    assert recovered.values.get("run_id") == run_id
88	    assert recovered.next  # still parked at the Gate 1 interrupt
89	
90	
91	def test_get_checkpointer_runs_setup_once_per_conn_str(monkeypatch):
92	    # #276 (pass-5): setup() must run once per process per conn_str, not on every
93	    # get_checkpointer() call. Counts real setup() invocations across two calls.
94	    _require_postgres()
95	    from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
96	
97	    import src.checkpointer as cp_mod
98	
99	    cp_mod._INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS.clear()
100	    calls = {"n": 0}
101	    orig = PostgresSaver.setup
102	
103	    def counting(self):
104	        calls["n"] += 1
105	        return orig(self)
106	
107	    monkeypatch.setattr(PostgresSaver, "setup", counting)
108	    get_checkpointer(use_memory=False)
109	    get_checkpointer(use_memory=False)
110	    assert calls["n"] == 1  # second call hits the _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS guard
111	
112	
113	def test_get_checkpointer_raises_helpful_error_on_connection_failure(monkeypatch):
114	    # The error path (lines wrapping ConnectionPool/setup) must surface the
115	    # operator-facing RuntimeError with the "is Postgres running" guidance. Mocks
116	    # ConnectionPool so it needs no real database.
117	    import psycopg_pool
118	
119	    def boom(*a, **k):
120	        raise RuntimeError("connection refused")
121	
122	    monkeypatch.setattr(psycopg_pool, "ConnectionPool", boom)
123	    with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="PostgresSaver connection failed"):
124	        get_checkpointer(use_memory=False)
125	
126	
127	def test_connection_string_rejects_remote_env_url(monkeypatch):
128	    # #6/#11 (pass-6): HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL bypasses config-load validation, so a
129	    # remote host must be rejected at the point of use (ADR-0002).
130	    from src.infrastructure import config
131	    monkeypatch.setenv("HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL", "postgresql://remote.example.com:5432/hcgrc")
132	    with pytest.raises(config.ConfigError, match="not loopback"):
133	        get_postgres_connection_string()
134	
135	
136	def test_connection_string_accepts_loopback_env_url(monkeypatch):
137	    monkeypatch.setenv("HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL", "postgresql://localhost:5432/hcgrc")
138	    assert get_postgres_connection_string() == "postgresql://localhost:5432/hcgrc"
139	
140	
141	def test_safe_close_swallows_errors_and_continues():
142	    # #3 (pass-6): one pool whose close() raises must not abort cleanup of the rest.
143	    import src.checkpointer as cp_mod
144	    closed = []
145	
146	    class _Pool:
147	        def __init__(self, bad):
148	            self.bad = bad
149	
150	        def close(self):
151	            if self.bad:
152	                raise RuntimeError("close boom")
153	            closed.append(self)
154	
155	    pools = [_Pool(bad=True), _Pool(bad=False)]
156	    # Mirror the registered atexit handler; the good pool must still be closed.
157	    [cp_mod._safe_close(p) for p in pools]
158	    assert len(closed) == 1
159	
160	
161	def test_get_checkpointer_setup_once_under_concurrency(monkeypatch):
162	    # #8 (pass-6): _init_lock must make setup() run exactly once even when many
163	    # threads call get_checkpointer() simultaneously.
164	    _require_postgres()
165	    import threading
166	
167	    from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
168	
169	    import src.checkpointer as cp_mod
170	
171	    cp_mod._INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS.clear()
172	    calls = {"n": 0}
173	    count_lock = threading.Lock()
174	    orig = PostgresSaver.setup
175	
176	    def counting(self):
177	        with count_lock:
178	            calls["n"] += 1
179	        return orig(self)
180	
181	    monkeypatch.setattr(PostgresSaver, "setup", counting)
182	    barrier = threading.Barrier(5)
183	    results = []
184	
185	    def worker():
186	        barrier.wait()  # release all threads at once
187	        results.append(get_checkpointer(use_memory=False))
188	
189	    threads = [threading.Thread(target=worker) for _ in range(5)]
190	    for t in threads:
191	        t.start()
192	    for t in threads:
193	        t.join()
194	    assert calls["n"] == 1  # setup ran exactly once despite 5 concurrent callers
195	    assert len(results) == 5

1	"""
2	reasoning_client — the ADR-0016 LLM execution-routing seam.
3	
4	A single abstraction that isolates LLM auth/transport from agent logic. Agents
5	declare *what* to reason about and at *what stakes tier*; this seam decides
6	*where* the call runs, how it authenticates, how it retries, times out, and how
7	it is traced. No agent imports an LLM client directly — that coupling lives here
8	and only here (ADR-0016 §"Implementation seam").
9	
10	Tiers (routing is by stakes, per ADR-0016 / agents/MODEL_TIER_ASSIGNMENTS.md):
11	
12	    Tier.T1  — Deterministic ML. No LLM. complete() on T1 is a routing bug and
13	               raises: T1 work is computation, never inference.
14	    Tier.T2  — Local LLM on the Mac mini, 24/7 (Ollama). Bulk / low-stakes
15	               reasoning: per-pair classification, triage, drafting, review.
16	    Tier.T3  — Frontier Claude Max 20x via claude-agent-sdk. The `claude` CLI's
17	               subscription OAuth (CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN) is the ONLY auth path
18	               — NO Anthropic API key. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is scrubbed from the SDK
19	               subprocess env so a stray key can never spill to the metered API
20	               (ADR-0016). Reserved for high-stakes judgments. Bursty / queued.
21	
22	Reliability (hardening pass 1):
23	  - Every call has a per-tier timeout (#145, #158) so a hung backend can never
24	    block a synchronous LangGraph node forever.
25	  - Transient failures are retried with bounded exponential backoff (#157).
26	  - Every call is wrapped in an OpenTelemetry span carrying run_id/agent_id/tier
27	    (OpenInference LLM conventions) so BOTH tiers are visible in Phoenix — T3 is
28	    not LangChain-instrumented, so without this it would be invisible (#150, #168).
29	    Spans are cheap no-ops when no tracer provider is registered (offline/tests).
30	  - complete_json uses Ollama's native JSON mode on T2 and a balanced-brace
31	    extractor, instead of a greedy regex that mangled multi-value replies (#159).
32	
33	Data sovereignty (ADR-0002 as amended by ADR-0016): persistent artifacts never
34	leave the machine; T3 permits transient, non-retained inference over the specific
35	SCF-derived text needed for a single call.
36	
37	Public surface:
38	    complete(tier, prompt, system=..., run_id=..., agent_id=...) -> str
39	    complete_json(tier, prompt, ...) -> dict | list
40	    is_available(tier) -> bool
41	"""
42	
43	from __future__ import annotations
44	
45	import asyncio
46	import json
47	import os
48	import threading
49	import time
50	from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
51	from concurrent.futures import TimeoutError as FuturesTimeout
52	from enum import Enum
53	from functools import lru_cache
54	from typing import Any
55	
56	from opentelemetry import trace
57	
58	# ── Tiers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
59	
60	
61	class Tier(str, Enum):
62	    """Execution tier, by stakes. See agents/MODEL_TIER_ASSIGNMENTS.md."""
63	
64	    T1 = "t1"  # deterministic ML — no LLM
65	    T2 = "t2"  # local Ollama
66	    T3 = "t3"  # frontier Claude Max via Agent SDK
67	
68	
69	class ReasoningError(RuntimeError):
70	    """Raised when a reasoning call cannot be completed (backend down, timeout, T1 misuse)."""
71	
72	
73	class PermanentReasoningError(ReasoningError):
74	    """A non-transient failure (model missing, auth, CLI absent). Never retried."""
75	
76	
77	class RateLimitError(ReasoningError):
78	    """A Tier-3 subscription rate window. Retried with long backpressure, never
79	    spilled to a metered API (ADR-0016)."""
80	
81	
82	# ── Configuration (env-overridable; local-first defaults) ─────────────────────
83	
84	
85	def _env(name: str, default: str) -> str:
86	    val = os.environ.get(name, "").strip()
87	    return val or default
88	
89	
90	T2_MODEL = _env("HCGRC_T2_MODEL", "llama3.1:8b")
91	T2_BASE_URL = _env("HCGRC_OLLAMA_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:11434")
92	T3_MODEL = os.environ.get("HCGRC_T3_MODEL", "").strip() or None
93	
94	# Per-tier hard timeouts (seconds). T3 is bursty/queued so it gets more headroom.
95	T2_TIMEOUT = float(_env("HCGRC_T2_TIMEOUT", "120"))
96	T3_TIMEOUT = float(_env("HCGRC_T3_TIMEOUT", "300"))
97	
98	# Tier-2 (Ollama) decoding controls. num_ctx avoids silent truncation at the 2048
99	# default (#196); seed makes greedy decoding reproducible (#197); num_predict caps
100	# output so a runaway/JSON-mode generation hits a bound before the hard timeout (#198).
101	T2_NUM_CTX = int(_env("HCGRC_T2_NUM_CTX", "8192"))
102	T2_SEED = int(_env("HCGRC_T2_SEED", "0"))
103	T2_NUM_PREDICT = int(_env("HCGRC_T2_NUM_PREDICT", "2048"))
104	
105	# Bounded retry with exponential backoff on transient failures. Clamped >= 0 so a
106	# negative override cannot produce an empty loop that raises None (#208).
107	MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("HCGRC_REASONING_RETRIES", "2")))
108	BACKOFF_BASE = float(_env("HCGRC_REASONING_BACKOFF", "1.5"))
109	# Tier-3 rate-window backpressure: long, capped waits — queue and resume, never
110	# fail fast onto a metered path.
111	RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF = float(_env("HCGRC_RATELIMIT_BACKOFF", "30"))
112	RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("HCGRC_RATELIMIT_RETRIES", "5")))
113	
114	# Truncation cap for prompt/response text recorded on spans (local Phoenix only).
115	_SPAN_TEXT_CAP = 8000
116	
117	_tracer = trace.get_tracer("hcgrc.reasoning_client")
118	
119	# Shared executor for enforcing sync-call timeouts. Not shut down: a hung backend
120	# call leaks one daemon worker rather than blocking the node (a ThreadPoolExecutor
121	# context-manager exit would join the hung thread, defeating the timeout).
122	_TIMEOUT_POOL_WORKERS = 8
123	_timeout_pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(
124	    max_workers=_TIMEOUT_POOL_WORKERS, thread_name_prefix="reasoning-timeout"
125	)
126	
127	# In-flight worker accounting (#272). A timed-out call abandons (does NOT cancel)
128	# its worker — ThreadPoolExecutor cannot interrupt a thread blocked in
129	# llm.invoke/asyncio.run — so a truly-hung backend leaks that slot until (if ever)
130	# the call returns. Without a bound, accumulated leaks saturate the 8-slot pool and
131	# every subsequent submit silently queues behind work that never starts, converting
132	# ALL traffic into guaranteed timeouts. We track in-flight slots and fail FAST and
133	# LOUD with a distinct error when the pool is saturated, instead of degrading into a
134	# silent cliff. The per-tier inner timeouts (T2 HTTP timeout, T3 asyncio.wait_for)
135	# normally let workers self-terminate, so this guard only fires under genuine leaks.
136	_inflight_lock = threading.Lock()
137	_inflight = 0
138	
139	
140	def _submit_bounded(fn):
141	    """Submit to the shared pool, refusing if all worker slots are already in
142	    flight (i.e. leaked to hung backends). Decrements the count when the future
143	    completes — a leaked worker keeps its slot counted until it finally returns."""
144	    global _inflight
145	    with _inflight_lock:
146	        if _inflight >= _TIMEOUT_POOL_WORKERS:
147	            raise ReasoningError(
148	                f"reasoning timeout pool saturated ({_inflight}/{_TIMEOUT_POOL_WORKERS} "
149	                "workers in-flight) — hung backend call(s) have leaked every worker slot"
150	            )
151	        _inflight += 1
152	
153	    def _release(_):
154	        global _inflight
155	        with _inflight_lock:
156	            _inflight -= 1
157	
158	    # Submit only after incrementing, but if submit() itself raises (e.g. the pool
159	    # was shut down — "cannot schedule new futures after shutdown") the done-callback
160	    # never fires, so decrement here to avoid permanently leaking the slot and
161	    # eventually false-saturating the pool (pass-5).
162	    try:
163	        future = _timeout_pool.submit(fn)
164	    except BaseException:
165	        with _inflight_lock:
166	            _inflight -= 1
167	        raise
168	    future.add_done_callback(_release)
169	    return future
170	
171	
172	# ── Timeout helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
173	
174	
175	def _with_timeout(fn, timeout: float, what: str):
176	    """Run a sync callable with a hard timeout; raise ReasoningError on overrun.
177	
178	    Propagates the current OpenTelemetry context into the worker thread so spans
179	    created by fn (e.g. the LangChain ChatOllama span) nest under the active
180	    reasoning.complete span instead of being orphaned (#238).
181	    """
182	    from opentelemetry import context as otel_context
183	
184	    ctx = otel_context.get_current()
185	
186	    def _run():
187	        token = otel_context.attach(ctx)
188	        try:
189	            return fn()
190	        finally:
191	            otel_context.detach(token)
192	
193	    future = _submit_bounded(_run)
194	    try:
195	        return future.result(timeout=timeout)
196	    except FuturesTimeout as exc:
197	        raise ReasoningError(f"{what} timed out after {timeout:.0f}s") from exc

200	def _run_sync(coro: Any) -> Any:
201	    """Run a coroutine to completion from sync code, even inside a running loop."""
202	    try:
203	        asyncio.get_running_loop()
204	    except RuntimeError:
205	        return asyncio.run(coro)
206	    # Bound the cross-thread wait so a starved/leaked pool can't deadlock a node
207	    # forever (#227). The inner coroutine already has its own asyncio timeout; this
208	    # is a backstop slightly beyond it. _submit_bounded fails fast if the pool is
209	    # already saturated with leaked workers rather than queueing forever (#272).
210	    return _submit_bounded(lambda: asyncio.run(coro)).result(timeout=T3_TIMEOUT + 30)

217	def _ollama(model: str, temperature: float, json_mode: bool) -> Any:
218	    """Construct (and cache) a ChatOllama client for the local serving stack."""
219	    try:
220	        from langchain_ollama import ChatOllama
221	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - dependency guard
222	        raise ReasoningError(
223	            "langchain-ollama is not installed — Tier 2 backend unavailable."
224	        ) from exc
225	    kwargs: dict[str, Any] = {
226	        "model": model,
227	        "temperature": temperature,
228	        "base_url": T2_BASE_URL,
229	        "num_ctx": T2_NUM_CTX,         # avoid silent truncation at the 2048 default (#196)
230	        "seed": T2_SEED,               # reproducible greedy decoding (#197)
231	        "client_kwargs": {"timeout": T2_TIMEOUT},  # native HTTP timeout (#195)
232	    }
233	    if json_mode:
234	        # Native structured-output: Ollama constrains decoding to valid JSON,
235	        # far more reliable than instructing + regex-extracting (#159, #175). Do
236	        # NOT cap num_predict here — format=json terminates at the end of a valid
237	        # JSON value, and a num_predict cap would truncate it into INVALID JSON
238	        # (#231). The hard timeout remains the runaway backstop.
239	        kwargs["format"] = "json"
240	    else:
241	        kwargs["num_predict"] = T2_NUM_PREDICT  # bound free-text output length (#198)
242	    return ChatOllama(**kwargs)

245	def _t2_complete(prompt: str, system: str | None, temperature: float, model: str,
246	                 json_mode: bool) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]:
247	    llm = _ollama(model, temperature, json_mode)
248	    messages: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
249	    if system:
250	        messages.append(("system", system))
251	    messages.append(("human", prompt))
252	
253	    def _call():
254	        resp = llm.invoke(messages)
255	        content = getattr(resp, "content", resp)
256	        text = content if isinstance(content, str) else str(content)
257	        return text, getattr(resp, "usage_metadata", None)  # token counts (#279)
258	
259	    try:
260	        # Give the outer future timeout margin over the inner HTTP timeout
261	        # (client_kwargs timeout == T2_TIMEOUT) so the inner socket timeout wins and
262	        # the worker self-terminates cleanly, instead of racing exactly and risking
263	        # an abandoned (leaked) worker — mirrors the T3_TIMEOUT + 30 backstop (#272).
264	        text, usage = _with_timeout(
265	            _call, T2_TIMEOUT + 10, f"Tier 2 (Ollama @ {T2_BASE_URL})"
266	        )
267	        text = text.strip()
268	        if not text:
269	            # An empty/whitespace reply is not a valid result — surface it as a
270	            # retryable error rather than silently returning "" and corrupting a
271	            # JSON caller or recording an empty high-stakes answer (#233).
272	            raise ReasoningError(f"Tier 2 (Ollama @ {T2_BASE_URL}) returned an empty reply")
273	        return text, {"model": model, "usage": usage}
274	    except ReasoningError:
275	        raise
276	    except Exception as exc:
277	        msg = str(exc).lower()
278	        # A missing model is permanent — retrying just wastes backoff (#194).
279	        if "not found" in msg or "no such model" in msg or "try pulling" in msg:
280	            raise PermanentReasoningError(
281	                f"Tier 2 model {model!r} not available on Ollama: {exc}"
282	            ) from exc
283	        raise ReasoningError(f"Tier 2 (Ollama @ {T2_BASE_URL}) call failed: {exc}") from exc

289	def _scrubbed_env() -> dict[str, str]:
290	    """
291	    Environment for the Agent SDK subprocess with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY removed.
292	
293	    ADR-0016: execution runs entirely on the fixed Claude Max subscription via the
294	    `claude` CLI's OAuth token. A metered Anthropic API key must NEVER be used — if
295	    one is present in the process env it would silently bill per-token, so we strip
296	    it before the SDK ever sees it.

298	    return {k: v for k, v in os.environ.items() if k != "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"}

454	    if cache_write is not None:

509	        # For T2, LangChainInstrumentor emits a child ChatOllama span under this

511	        # provider call), not redundant double-tracing (#214). T3 has no LangChain
512	        # span, so this is the only record of the frontier call.

654	def _extract_json(text: str) -> Any:
655	    """Recover a JSON value from a model reply that may include fences/prose."""
656	    candidate = text.strip()
657	    # Strip a ```json ... ``` fence if present.
658	    if candidate.startswith("```"):
659	        inner = candidate[3:]
660	        if inner[:4].lower() == "json":
661	            inner = inner[4:]
662	        candidate = inner.rsplit("```", 1)[0].strip()

664	        return json.loads(candidate)
665	    except json.JSONDecodeError:

667	    span = _first_balanced_json(candidate)
668	    if span is not None:
669	        try:
670	            return json.loads(span)
671	        except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
672	            raise ReasoningError(f"Reply was not valid JSON: {text[:200]!r}") from exc
673	    raise ReasoningError(f"No JSON value found in reply: {text[:200]!r}")

676	def _first_balanced_json(text: str) -> str | None:
677	    """
678	    Return the first balanced {...} or [...] span, respecting string literals.
679	
680	    A greedy regex (`[\\{\\[].*[\\}\\]]`) grabs from the first brace to the LAST
681	    brace, which mangles replies containing multiple JSON values or braces in
682	    trailing prose. This walks the structure honestly (#159).
683	    """
684	    start = None
685	    for i, ch in enumerate(text):
686	        if ch in "{[":
687	            start = i
688	            break
689	    if start is None:
690	        return None
691	    open_ch = text[start]
692	    close_ch = "}" if open_ch == "{" else "]"
693	    depth = 0
694	    in_str = False
695	    escaped = False
696	    for i in range(start, len(text)):
697	        ch = text[i]
698	        if in_str:
699	            if escaped:
700	                escaped = False
701	            elif ch == "\\":
702	                escaped = True
703	            elif ch == '"':
704	                in_str = False
705	            continue
706	        if ch == '"':
707	            in_str = True
708	        elif ch == open_ch:
709	            depth += 1
710	        elif ch == close_ch:
711	            depth -= 1
712	            if depth == 0:
713	                return text[start:i + 1]
714	    return None
715	
716	
717	def is_available(tier: Tier) -> bool:
718	    """
719	    Cheap, side-effect-light liveness check for a tier's backend, used by nodes
720	    that must degrade gracefully rather than fail the run.
721	
722	    Does NOT spend an LLM turn (the old probe burned a real Tier-3 frontier turn
723	    on every call, #174, and could hang with no timeout, #173/#30). T1 is always
724	    available (local computation). Honors HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING.
725	    """
726	    if tier is Tier.T1:
727	        return True
728	    if os.environ.get("HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING", "").strip():
729	        return False
730	    if tier is Tier.T2:
731	        # Hit Ollama's /api/tags — no generation, short timeout — and confirm the
732	        # configured model is actually pulled, not merely that the server is up (#210).
733	        import json as _json
734	        import urllib.request
735	        try:
736	            with urllib.request.urlopen(f"{T2_BASE_URL}/api/tags", timeout=3) as r:
737	                if r.status != 200:
738	                    return False
739	                names = [m.get("name", "") for m in _json.loads(r.read()).get("models", [])]
740	        except Exception:
741	            return False
742	        # Exact match only: a prefix match (e.g. configured llama3.1:8b vs a
743	        # pulled llama3.1:70b) would pass here but complete() would then fail as
744	        # "model not found" (#232). Ollama tags an untagged pull as ":latest".
745	        return any(n == T2_MODEL or n == f"{T2_MODEL}:latest" for n in names)
746	    if tier is Tier.T3:
747	        # The `claude` CLI must be present and an OAuth token / stored creds
748	        # available — no frontier turn spent.
749	        import shutil
750	        if shutil.which("claude") is None:
751	            return False
752	        if os.environ.get("CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN", "").strip():
753	            return True
754	        creds = os.path.expanduser("~/.claude/.credentials.json")
755	        return os.path.exists(creds)
756	    return False

1	"""
2	PostgresSaver configuration for HC-GRC.
3	
4	LangGraph uses the checkpointer to persist graph state between turns,
5	enabling interrupt/resume across the operator approval gates.
6	
7	Per ADR-0015 (#79): run_id propagates to PostgresSaver as thread_id.
8	Cross-store query procedure in RUNBOOK.md §5.
9	
10	LOCAL-FIRST CONSTRAINT: PostgreSQL instance runs on-device.
11	No state leaves the machine. SaaS checkpointers are disqualified.
12	
13	Connection config:
14	  Database: hcgrc
15	  Host: localhost (default)
16	  Port: 5432 (default)
17	
18	For Phase 0 testing: use MemorySaver (no Postgres required).
19	For Phase 1+: use PostgresSaver with the connection string below.
20	"""
21	
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	import atexit
25	import os
26	import threading
27	
28	from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver
29	
30	# Arbitrary fixed key for the Postgres session advisory lock that serializes
31	# checkpoint schema migrations across concurrent initializers (get_checkpointer).
32	_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY = 0x4843_4752  # "HCGR"
33	
34	# Guards the process-local one-time-init bookkeeping below. The PG advisory lock
35	# serializes the DDL across PROCESSES, but the in-memory check-and-set guards
36	# (_atexit_registered, _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS) are not atomic on their own — two
37	# threads calling get_checkpointer() concurrently could both pass the check and
38	# double-register the atexit handler / double-run setup(). This lock makes those
39	# check-and-set sequences atomic within the process (pass-5).
40	_init_lock = threading.Lock()
41	
42	# Pools opened by get_checkpointer, closed once at process exit. A single atexit
43	# handler closes them all — registering one per call would accumulate handlers
44	# across repeated get_checkpointer() calls (#249).
45	_OPEN_POOLS: list = []
46	_atexit_registered = False
47	
48	# conn_strs whose schema bootstrap (advisory lock + setup() DDL + migration SELECT)
49	# has already run in this process. setup() is idempotent but not free — re-running
50	# it on every get_checkpointer() call takes a global advisory lock and replays the
51	# migration version query at each run start. Bootstrap once per process per schema;
52	# the advisory lock still guards the first-time cross-process migration race (#276).
53	_INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS: set[str] = set()
54	
55	
56	def _safe_close(pool) -> None:
57	    """Close one pool, swallowing errors so a single failing close() does not abort
58	    cleanup of the remaining pools at interpreter exit (pass-6). close() ultimately
59	    calls a C finish() that can raise."""
60	    try:
61	        pool.close()
62	    except Exception:
63	        pass
64	
65	
66	def _register_pool_cleanup(pool) -> None:
67	    global _atexit_registered
68	    with _init_lock:
69	        _OPEN_POOLS.append(pool)
70	        if not _atexit_registered:
71	            atexit.register(lambda: [_safe_close(p) for p in _OPEN_POOLS])
72	            _atexit_registered = True
73	
74	# ── Connection string ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
75	
76	def get_postgres_connection_string() -> str:
77	    """
78	    Return the PostgreSQL connection string.
79	
80	    Precedence: HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL env var → the checkpointing.{host,port,database}
81	    section of platform.yaml → localhost default. This makes the platform.yaml
82	    checkpointing config live instead of dead (#225). Never logged, never committed.
83	
84	    ADR-0002: the resolved URI MUST point at a loopback host — graph state holds the
85	    full corpus analysis / hypotheses / gate decisions and must not leave the box.
86	    The env-var path bypasses config-load validation, so it is guarded here too
87	    (point-of-use defense-in-depth, mirroring Qdrant/MLflow) (pass-6).
88	    """
89	    from .infrastructure.config import require_local_uri
90	
91	    env_url = os.environ.get("HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL")
92	    if env_url:
93	        return require_local_uri(env_url, "checkpointing (HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL)")
94	    try:
95	        from .infrastructure.config import load_platform_config
96	        cp = load_platform_config().get("checkpointing", {}) or {}
97	        host = cp.get("host", "localhost")
98	        port = cp.get("port", 5432)
99	        database = cp.get("database", "hcgrc")
100	        url = f"postgresql://{host}:{port}/{database}"
101	    except Exception:
102	        return "postgresql://localhost:5432/hcgrc"
103	    # Validate OUTSIDE the try so a non-loopback host raises rather than being
104	    # masked by the localhost fallback.
105	    return require_local_uri(url, "checkpointing")
106	
107	
108	def configured_checkpointer():
109	    """
110	    Return the checkpointer the platform config asks for: MemorySaver when
111	    checkpointing.backend == 'memory', else the pooled PostgresSaver (#226).
112	    Runners use this as the default instead of hardcoding in-memory.
113	    """
114	    backend = "postgres"
115	    try:
116	        from .infrastructure.config import load_platform_config
117	        backend = (load_platform_config().get("checkpointing", {}) or {}).get("backend", "postgres")
118	    except Exception:
119	        backend = "postgres"
120	    return get_checkpointer(use_memory=(backend == "memory"))
121	
122	
123	# ── Checkpointer factory ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
124	
125	def get_checkpointer(use_memory: bool = False):
126	    """
127	    Return a configured checkpointer.
128	
129	    Args:
130	        use_memory: If True, return MemorySaver (Phase 0 testing, no Postgres needed).
131	                    If False, return PostgresSaver (Phase 1+ production).
132	
133	    Returns:
134	        MemorySaver | PostgresSaver
135	    """
136	    if use_memory:
137	        return MemorySaver()
138	
139	    try:
140	        from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
141	        from psycopg.rows import dict_row
142	        from psycopg_pool import ConnectionPool
143	    except ImportError as e:
144	        raise ImportError(
145	            "langgraph-checkpoint-postgres and psycopg[pool] are required for PostgresSaver. "
146	            "Run: pip install 'langgraph-checkpoint-postgres' 'psycopg[binary,pool]'"
147	        ) from e
148	
149	    conn_str = get_postgres_connection_string()
150	    try:
151	        # A single raw Connection has no recovery: when the Postgres backend goes
152	        # away (node sleep/restart, idle timeout, network blip) the cached
153	        # connection is permanently broken and every subsequent checkpoint op
154	        # raises — silent loss of durability exactly during interrupt/resume across
155	        # the approval gates. Use a self-healing ConnectionPool instead: it runs a
156	        # liveness check on checkout and discards/replaces dead connections.
157	        # PostgresSaver natively accepts a ConnectionPool. (Hardening pass 1, #4.)
158	        pool = ConnectionPool(
159	            conn_str,
160	            min_size=1,
161	            max_size=8,
162	            kwargs={
163	                "autocommit": True,
164	                "prepare_threshold": 0,
165	                "row_factory": dict_row,
166	            },
167	            check=ConnectionPool.check_connection,
168	            open=True,
169	        )
170	        # PostgresSaver does not own the pool, so close it cleanly at process exit
171	        # (otherwise the pool's worker thread cannot be joined during interpreter
172	        # finalization, raising a noisy PythonFinalizationError). A single shared
173	        # atexit handler closes all pools — no per-call accumulation (#249).
174	        _register_pool_cleanup(pool)
175	        # Serialize schema migrations across processes with a session advisory
176	        # lock (pass-1 #33). The lock and the migration DDL MUST run on the SAME
177	        # connection — pg_advisory_lock is session-scoped, and a pool-backed
178	        # PostgresSaver.setup() would check out a *different* connection for the
179	        # DDL, leaving the lock ineffective (pass-3 #2). So run setup() through a
180	        # saver bound to the single locked connection, then return the pool-backed
181	        # saver for normal operation.
182	        # Bootstrap the schema once per process per conn_str. setup() is idempotent
183	        # but a redundant advisory lock + migration SELECT on every run start is
184	        # wasteful; the lock still protects the first-time cross-process race (#276).
185	        # _init_lock makes the in-process check-and-set atomic so concurrent threads
186	        # don't both run setup() (pass-5); the held lock also serializes the DDL,
187	        # which is fine — it runs at most once per conn_str per process.
188	        with _init_lock:
189	            if conn_str not in _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS:
190	                with pool.connection() as conn:
191	                    conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_lock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
192	                    try:
193	                        PostgresSaver(conn).setup()  # DDL on the locked connection
194	                    finally:
195	                        conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
196	                _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS.add(conn_str)
197	        return PostgresSaver(pool)
198	    except Exception as e:
199	        raise RuntimeError(
200	            f"PostgresSaver connection failed. Is Postgres running on {conn_str}? "
201	            f"Error: {e}\n"
202	            "For Phase 0 testing without Postgres, use get_checkpointer(use_memory=True)."
203	        ) from e
204

1	"""
2	Gate nodes for the HC-GRC research platform.
3	
4	Five gates enforce human approval at structural transition points.
5	Each gate issues a LangGraph interrupt(), surfacing a structured proposal
6	to the operator. The operator responds via Claude on any device (ADR-0014).
7	
8	Gate decisions are written by gate_coordinator_node — never directly here.
9	On rejection, each gate appends a failure_event for Agent Evolution monitoring.
10	
11	Gate definitions:
12	  Gate 1 — Pre-data checkpoint (Phase 0 complete, all prerequisites verified)
13	  Gate 2 — Exploratory → Confirmatory (hypothesis lock, test data unlock)
14	  Gate 3 — Finding significance review (before publication pipeline)
15	  Gate 4 — Manuscript submission approval
16	  Gate 5 — Escalation resolution gate (triggered by ADR-0014 Escalation loop)
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import datetime
22	from typing import Any
23	
24	from langgraph.types import interrupt
25	
26	from ..state import HCGRCState
27	
28	# ── Shared helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
29	
30	
31	def _build_gate_proposal(gate_id: str, title: str, summary: str,
32	                          checklist: list[str], run_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
33	    """Build the structured proposal surfaced to the operator via interrupt()."""
34	    return {
35	        "gate_id": gate_id,
36	        "title": title,
37	        "summary": summary,
38	        "checklist": checklist,
39	        "run_id": run_id,
40	        "timestamp_utc": datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc).isoformat(),
41	        "instructions": (
42	            "Respond with: {'decision': 'approved'|'rejected'|'deferred', "
43	            "'rationale': '<your reasoning>'}"
44	        ),
45	    }
46	
47	
48	def _rejection_event(gate_id: str, rationale: str, run_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
49	    """Failure event appended to state for Agent Evolution monitoring."""
50	    return {
51	        "event_type": "gate_rejection",
52	        "gate_id": gate_id,
53	        "rationale": rationale,
54	        "run_id": run_id,
55	        "timestamp_utc": datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc).isoformat(),
56	    }
57	
58	
59	_VALID_DECISIONS = {"approved", "rejected", "deferred"}
60	# Only 'approved' is terminal — it stops the gate re-firing on re-entry (don't
61	# re-unlock). 'rejected' routes back for re-review and 'deferred' parks the run for
62	# the operator to re-trigger later; BOTH must re-fire the interrupt when the gate
63	# is re-entered, so neither is terminal (pass-3 #5).
64	_TERMINAL_DECISIONS = {"approved"}
65	
66	
67	def _parse_operator_response(resp: Any, gate_id: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
68	    """
69	    Validate the operator's interrupt() response and return (decision, rationale).
70	
71	    A malformed response must NOT be silently coerced to 'rejected' (that would
72	    let a non-dict or a typo'd decision masquerade as a real governance decision,
73	    #163/#180). Fail loud so the operator re-submits a well-formed response on the
74	    next resume.
75	    """
76	    if not isinstance(resp, dict):
77	        raise ValueError(
78	            f"{gate_id}: operator response must be a dict like "
79	            f"{{'decision': 'approved'|'rejected'|'deferred', 'rationale': '...'}}, "
80	            f"got {type(resp).__name__}."
81	        )
82	    decision = resp.get("decision")
83	    if decision not in _VALID_DECISIONS:
84	        raise ValueError(
85	            f"{gate_id}: operator response 'decision' must be one of "
86	            f"{sorted(_VALID_DECISIONS)}, got {decision!r}."
87	        )
88	    rationale = resp.get("rationale") or "No rationale provided."
89	    return decision, rationale
90	
91	
92	def _already_decided(state: HCGRCState, gate_id: str) -> bool:
93	    """
94	    True if this gate already holds a TERMINAL decision (approved only).
95	
96	    Guards against a re-invocation re-firing the interrupt and re-running
97	    downstream effects on top of a terminal decision (#177). A 'rejected' record
98	    is deliberately NOT terminal: the Gate-2 reject → revise → re-review loop
99	    (route_after_gate_2 → exploratory → … → gate_2) must re-fire the interrupt so
100	    the operator is re-prompted, otherwise the gate short-circuits to {} and the
101	    graph spins until GraphRecursionError (pass-2 #184/#187). The loop-back path
102	    is responsible for producing the revised state that warrants re-review.
103	    """
104	    record = state.get("gate_status", {}).get(gate_id)
105	    return bool(record) and record.get("decision") in _TERMINAL_DECISIONS
106	
107	
108	def _finalize_gate(state: HCGRCState, gate_id: str, decision: str,
109	                   rationale: str, reviewer: str = "operator") -> dict[str, Any]:
110	    """Build the gate node's state update: failure event (if not approved) + the
111	    authoritative gate_status record (written only by gate_coordinator_node)."""
112	    update: dict[str, Any] = {}
113	    if decision == "rejected":
114	        # 'deferred' is a park, not a failure — only a rejection is a failure_event
115	        # (a deferred decision recorded as a rejection mislabels the audit trail).
116	        update["failure_events"] = [_rejection_event(gate_id, rationale, state["run_id"])]
117	    from .gate_coordinator import gate_coordinator_node
118	    update.update(gate_coordinator_node(state, gate_id, decision, rationale, reviewer=reviewer))
119	    return update
120	
121	
122	def _run_gate(state: HCGRCState, gate_id: str, proposal: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
123	    """
124	    Drive one gate: idempotency guard, then interrupt() the operator and finalize.
125	
126	    Validation runs INSIDE a re-prompt loop (pass-3 #3): interrupt() consumes and
127	    persists the resume value before validation, so raising on a malformed value
128	    would strand it in the checkpoint and replay it forever. Instead, a malformed
129	    or mis-correlated response re-fires a fresh interrupt (next resume index)
130	    carrying the error, so the operator can re-submit and actually recover.
131	
132	    Gate-id correlation (pass-3 #4): if the operator response names a gate, it must
133	    match this gate — a stale/misrouted decision cannot be applied to the wrong gate.
134	    """
135	    if _already_decided(state, gate_id):
136	        return {}
137	    current = proposal
138	    while True:
139	        resp = interrupt(current)
140	        if isinstance(resp, dict) and resp.get("gate_id") not in (None, gate_id):
141	            current = {**proposal, "error": (
142	                f"response gate_id {resp.get('gate_id')!r} does not match {gate_id}")}
143	            continue
144	        try:
145	            decision, rationale = _parse_operator_response(resp, gate_id)
146	        except ValueError as exc:
147	            current = {**proposal, "error": str(exc)}
148	            continue
149	        return _finalize_gate(state, gate_id, decision, rationale)
150	
151	
152	# ── Gate 1 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
153	
154	
155	def gate_1_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
156	    """
157	    Gate 1 — Pre-data checkpoint.
158	
159	    Fires before any SCF data enters the system. The operator verifies that
160	    all Phase 0 prerequisites are complete (ADR-0015, #71):
161	
162	      1. LangGraph skeleton running
163	      2. Gate 1 fires with synthetic payload (this test)
164	      3. Data split idempotency verified (SHA-256 seed reproducible)
165	      4. PostgresSaver benchmark complete (throughput on target hardware)
166	      5. Governance dry-run complete (full escalation loop exercised)
167	      6. Diversity anchor models identified (T-01, ADR-0013)
168	
169	    The interrupt() surfaces the proposal and parks the graph until the
170	    operator responds. No SCF data is loaded before this gate approves.
171	    """
172	    checklist = [
173	        "LangGraph skeleton compiles and graph executes on synthetic payload",
174	        "Gate 1 interrupt fires and resolves correctly",
175	        "compute_data_split() produces identical splits on two independent runs (SHA-256 seed)",
176	        "PostgresSaver write/read benchmark complete — throughput logged to MLflow",
177	        "Governance dry-run complete — escalation loop exercised end-to-end",
178	        # ── Preregistration Ledger entries (logged before Phase 1 data ingestion) ──
179	        "LEDGER-0002: Diversity anchor model candidates logged (T-01 — 3 general + 2 anchors)",
180	        "LEDGER-0003: Fleiss kappa path selection logged (path 1: recruit raters | path 2: acknowledge unestimated)",
181	        # ── NOTE: DIVERGENCE-01 is a Gate 2 prerequisite, NOT Gate 1 ──────────────
182	        # DIVERGENCE-01 (operationalization of model-STRM divergence) requires the
183	        # STRM label distribution from EDA — which has not run yet at Gate 1.
184	        # Decision framework is defined in SAP §10. Specific operationalization is
185	        # selected post-EDA, logged to Preregistration Ledger before Gate 2.
186	        # Confirmed: SAP §10 DIVERGENCE-01 decision framework understood and acknowledged",
187	    ]
188	
189	    proposal = _build_gate_proposal(
190	        gate_id="gate_1",
191	        title="Gate 1 — Pre-Data Checkpoint",
192	        summary=(
193	            "Platform has completed Phase 0 verification. All structural prerequisites "
194	            "are in place before SCF corpus data enters the system. "
195	            "Approve to unlock data ingestion and begin Phase 1."
196	        ),
197	        checklist=checklist,
198	        run_id=state["run_id"],
199	    )
200	
201	    # interrupt() parks the graph and surfaces proposal to operator.
202	    # The operator's response dict is the return value of interrupt().
203	    return _run_gate(state, "gate_1", proposal)
204	
205	
206	# ── Gate 2 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
207	
208	
209	def gate_2_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
210	    """
211	    Gate 2 — Exploratory → Confirmatory transition.
212	
213	    The most consequential gate. The operator reviews the pre-registered
214	    hypothesis set and approves unlocking the test split. Uses ADR-0014
215	    Escalation infrastructure (GitHub issue → Claude → decision record).
216	
217	    Hard prerequisites (checked before interrupt fires):
218	      - DIVERGENCE-01 operationalization logged to Preregistration Ledger
219	      - Primary embedding model designated and logged
220	      - Hypothesis set complete and committed to PREREGISTRATION_LEDGER.md
221	      - Data split idempotency verified (data_split_verified == True)
222	    """
223	    # Verify hard prerequisites before surfacing this irreversible gate. Gate 2
224	    # unlocks the test split, so the machine-checkable preconditions for a sound
225	    # exploratory→confirmatory transition are ALL enforced here, not just the data
226	    # split (pass-3 #1): exploratory analysis must be complete and a non-empty
227	    # pre-registered hypothesis set must exist before the operator can even be asked.
228	    hard_failures = []
229	    if not state.get("data_split_verified"):
230	        hard_failures.append("data_split_verified is False — run compute_data_split() first")
231	    if not state.get("exploratory_complete"):
232	        hard_failures.append(
233	            "exploratory_complete is False — P1-P5 exploratory analysis has not finished")
234	    if not state.get("hypothesis_set"):
235	        hard_failures.append(
236	            "hypothesis_set is empty — no pre-registered hypotheses to lock at Gate 2")
237	
238	    if hard_failures:
239	        # Write an authoritative gate_status record so the router and operator see
240	        # the failure (#164/#178). Decision is DEFERRED, not rejected: a system
241	        # prerequisite failure must PARK the run (route_after_gate_2 deferred→END),
242	        # not feed it into the operator reject→revise→re-review loop, which would
243	        # spin forever because re-running exploratory cannot satisfy an unmet
244	        # prerequisite (pass-3 #20). The operator re-triggers once prereqs are met.
245	        rationale = "Hard prerequisite failure: " + "; ".join(hard_failures)
246	        # 'deferred' is non-terminal, so this path re-runs whenever gate_2 is
247	        # re-entered (operator re-trigger after the park, or the reject→exploratory
248	        # →…→gate_2 loop) while prereqs are still unmet. failure_events has a plain
249	        # append reducer (state.py:86), so re-appending an identical
250	        # gate_prerequisite_failure on every re-entry duplicates the audit record
251	        # and biases the Agent Evolution failure-count signal. Only append when this
252	        # exact deferred prerequisite failure is not already recorded (#267).
253	        prior = state.get("gate_status", {}).get("gate_2") or {}
254	        already_recorded = (
255	            prior.get("decision") == "deferred" and prior.get("rationale") == rationale
256	        )
257	        update: dict[str, Any] = {}
258	        if not already_recorded:
259	            update["failure_events"] = [
260	                {
261	                    "event_type": "gate_prerequisite_failure",
262	                    "gate_id": "gate_2",
263	                    "failures": hard_failures,
264	                    "run_id": state["run_id"],

1	"""
2	Phoenix observability bootstrap for HC-GRC (roadmap Phase 0, deliverable #2).
3	
4	ADR-0009: Phoenix is the local, self-hosted LLM-tracing stack (LangSmith was
5	disqualified as SaaS-only). The Phoenix server runs on the compute node and
6	agent LLM calls export OpenTelemetry traces to it.
7	
8	Two responsibilities, deliberately separated:
9	
10	  1. The SERVER is a long-lived process — run it via the `phoenix serve` CLI
11	     (see launch_server_command()) or the launchd plist in scripts/. It is NOT
12	     launched from inside the application process.
13	
14	  2. INSTRUMENTATION is wired per-process via bootstrap_observability(), called
15	     once by each run entrypoint. It registers a global OTel tracer provider
16	     (BatchSpanProcessor — spans are buffered and exported in the background, so
17	     a momentary server outage drops spans rather than blocking or failing the
18	     run) pointed at the running server, and turns on the LangChain instrumentor.
19	     Because the provider is registered globally, the spans the reasoning_client
20	     emits for BOTH tiers — including Tier-3 (claude-agent-sdk), which LangChain
21	     instrumentation does not see — export to Phoenix too.
22	
23	run_id propagation (ADR-0015 #79): wrap a run in run_trace_context(run_id) so
24	every span produced during the run carries the run_id as session.id + metadata,
25	cross-referencing MLflow / PostgresSaver / PROV-DM.
26	"""
27	
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	import atexit
31	import logging
32	import os
33	import signal
34	import sys
35	import threading
36	from contextlib import contextmanager
37	
38	# ADR-0002: traces carry SCF-derived content and must never leave the machine.
39	# The loopback guard is the single canonical validator in config.py, shared with
40	# Qdrant and MLflow (pass-5) so the data-sovereignty boundary is enforced
41	# identically everywhere; 0.0.0.0 is rejected (it binds all interfaces).
42	from ..config import LOOPBACK_HOSTS as _LOOPBACK_HOSTS  # noqa: F401  (kept for callers/tests)
43	from ..config import load_platform_config
44	from ..config import require_loopback as _require_loopback
45	
46	_log = logging.getLogger(__name__)
47	
48	# Cached registered provider — registration is idempotent within a process
49	# (repeat bootstrap calls return the same provider instead of double-registering
50	# the instrumentor, which would duplicate every span).
51	_PROVIDER = None
52	
53	
54	def _phoenix_cfg() -> dict:
55	    return load_platform_config()["observability"]["phoenix"]
56	
57	
58	def phoenix_base_url() -> str:
59	    """Return the Phoenix server base URL, e.g. http://localhost:6006."""
60	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
61	    return f"http://{cfg.get('host', 'localhost')}:{cfg.get('port', 6006)}"
62	
63	
64	def phoenix_endpoint() -> str:
65	    """Return the OTLP/HTTP traces endpoint the tracer provider exports to.
66	
67	    Validates loopback at the source (pass-5 #obs-wiring): this is public API and
68	    is called from the __main__ entrypoint and tests, not only from the already-
69	    guarded instrument_langchain(), so it must enforce its own ADR-0002 precondition."""
70	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
71	    _require_loopback(str(cfg.get("host", "localhost")), "Phoenix OTLP export")
72	    return f"{phoenix_base_url()}/v1/traces"
73	
74	
75	def launch_server_command() -> str:
76	    """Return the shell command that starts the persistent Phoenix server.
77	
78	    Validates the bind host is loopback (#283): the server bind, not just the
79	    client export host, is what determines whether traces are reachable off-box.
80	    """
81	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
82	    host = _require_loopback(str(cfg.get("host", "localhost")), "server bind")
83	    return f"phoenix serve --host {host} --port {cfg.get('port', 6006)}"
84	
85	
86	def instrument_langchain(project_name: str | None = None):
87	    """
88	    Register a global OTel tracer provider pointed at the Phoenix server (using a
89	    background BatchSpanProcessor) and enable LangChain tracing for this process.
90	
91	    Idempotent: the first call registers; subsequent calls return the cached
92	    provider without re-instrumenting (so spans are never duplicated).
93	
94	    Returns the tracer provider.
95	    """
96	    global _PROVIDER
97	    if _PROVIDER is not None:
98	        return _PROVIDER
99	
100	    from openinference.instrumentation.langchain import LangChainInstrumentor
101	    from opentelemetry import trace as _trace
102	    from phoenix.otel import register
103	
104	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
105	    # ADR-0002 data sovereignty: refuse to export SCF-derived traces off-box.
106	    _require_loopback(str(cfg.get("host", "localhost")), "OTLP export")
107	
108	    # Bound the OTLP/HTTP exporter so a downed Phoenix server cannot block process
109	    # teardown for the exporter's default 10s on the shutdown flush. The installed
110	    # OTel BatchProcessor.force_flush() ignores its timeout_millis argument, so the
111	    # only effective cap is at the exporter level (#277). Don't clobber an explicit
112	    # operator override.
113	    os.environ.setdefault("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_TIMEOUT", "2")
114	
115	    provider = register(
116	        project_name=project_name or cfg.get("project_name", "hc-grc"),
117	        endpoint=phoenix_endpoint(),
118	        batch=True,                        # BatchSpanProcessor: buffer + async export
119	        set_global_tracer_provider=True,   # so reasoning_client spans export too
120	    )
121	    # set_tracer_provider can only succeed once per process; if a competing global
122	    # provider already won the race, register() still RETURNS the Phoenix provider
123	    # (so LangChain spans, passed it explicitly, still reach Phoenix) but the GLOBAL
124	    # provider is something else — and reasoning_client's module-level tracer resolves
125	    # through the global, so Tier-3 frontier spans (their ONLY record) would silently
126	    # vanish. Surface that collision instead of dropping them silently (#278).
127	    if _trace.get_tracer_provider() is not provider:
128	        _log.warning(
129	            "Phoenix did not become the global OTel tracer provider — a competing "
130	            "provider was registered first. Tier-3 reasoning_client spans will not "
131	            "reach Phoenix. Ensure bootstrap_observability() runs before any other "
132	            "OTel instrumentation."
133	        )
134	    LangChainInstrumentor().instrument(tracer_provider=provider, skip_dep_check=True)
135	    # Flush buffered spans at process exit so the BatchSpanProcessor does not drop
136	    # the tail of a run on a clean exit / SIGINT (#215). atexit does NOT run on
137	    # SIGTERM (launchd/kill/container-stop), so also install a SIGTERM handler that
138	    # flushes then exits — otherwise the tail of a killed run is silently lost
139	    # (#281). Both flushes are bounded by the exporter timeout set above (#277).
140	    if hasattr(provider, "force_flush"):
141	        atexit.register(provider.force_flush)
142	        _install_sigterm_flush(provider)
143	    _PROVIDER = provider
144	    return provider
145	
146	
147	def _install_sigterm_flush(provider) -> None:
148	    """Flush + shut down the span processor on SIGTERM before the process exits.
149	
150	    Signal handlers can only be installed from the main thread; if we're not on it
151	    (e.g. instrumentation wired from a worker), skip silently — atexit still covers
152	    the clean-exit and SIGINT paths."""
153	    if threading.current_thread() is not threading.main_thread():
154	        return
155	
156	    def _handler(_signum, _frame):
157	        try:
158	            provider.force_flush()
159	            if hasattr(provider, "shutdown"):
160	                provider.shutdown()
161	        finally:
162	            sys.exit(0)
163	
164	    try:
165	        signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handler)
166	    except (ValueError, OSError):  # pragma: no cover - non-main-thread / unsupported
167	        pass
168	
169	
170	def bootstrap_observability():
171	    """
172	    Idempotently wire tracing for the current process. Called once by each run
173	    entrypoint (graph runners). Degrades to a no-op — never raising — when tracing
174	    is disabled by config, switched off via HCGRC_DISABLE_TRACING, or the
175	    instrumentation stack cannot be initialized. Returns the provider or None.
176	    """
177	    if os.environ.get("HCGRC_DISABLE_TRACING", "").strip():
178	        return None
179	    try:
180	        cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
181	    except Exception:
182	        return None
183	    if not cfg.get("enabled", True):
184	        return None
185	    # Also point MLflow at the local store so experiment tracking is actually
186	    # configured in a run path (cross-store run_id correlation, #282). Best-effort.
187	    try:
188	        from ..tracking.mlflow_setup import configure_mlflow
189	        configure_mlflow()
190	    except Exception:
191	        pass
192	    try:
193	        return instrument_langchain()
194	    except Exception:
195	        # Missing deps, server unreachable at registration, etc. — tracing is
196	        # best-effort observability and must never block the research run.
197	        return None
198	
199	
200	@contextmanager
201	def run_trace_context(run_id: str | None):
202	    """
203	    Context manager that stamps run_id onto every span produced within it, for
204	    cross-store correlation. Sets it two ways so BOTH span sources pick it up:
205	    OpenInference using_attributes (LangChain auto-spans → session.id) AND OTel
206	    baggage `hcgrc.run_id`, which the reasoning_client seam reads as a fallback so
207	    its manual T2/T3 spans carry the run_id even when the caller didn't pass it
208	    explicitly (#239). No-op if run_id is None or the helpers are unavailable.
209	    """
210	    if not run_id:
211	        yield
212	        return
213	    from contextlib import ExitStack
214	
215	    from opentelemetry import baggage, context
216	
217	    with ExitStack() as stack:
218	        try:
219	            from openinference.instrumentation import using_attributes
220	            stack.enter_context(using_attributes(session_id=run_id, metadata={"run_id": run_id}))
221	        except Exception:
222	            pass
223	        token = context.attach(baggage.set_baggage("hcgrc.run_id", run_id))
224	        try:
225	            yield
226	        finally:
227	            context.detach(token)
228	
229	
230	if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover - operational entrypoint
231	    print(f"Phoenix base URL:    {phoenix_base_url()}")
232	    print(f"OTLP traces endpoint: {phoenix_endpoint()}")
233	    print(f"Start the server with: {launch_server_command()}")

676	def _first_balanced_json(text: str) -> str | None:
677	    """
678	    Return the first balanced {...} or [...] span, respecting string literals.
679	
680	    A greedy regex (`[\\{\\[].*[\\}\\]]`) grabs from the first brace to the LAST
681	    brace, which mangles replies containing multiple JSON values or braces in
682	    trailing prose. This walks the structure honestly (#159).
683	    """
684	    start = None
685	    for i, ch in enumerate(text):
686	        if ch in "{[":
687	            start = i
688	            break
689	    if start is None:
690	        return None
691	    open_ch = text[start]
692	    close_ch = "}" if open_ch == "{" else "]"
693	    depth = 0
694	    in_str = False
695	    escaped = False
696	    for i in range(start, len(text)):
697	        ch = text[i]
698	        if in_str:
699	            if escaped:
700	                escaped = False
701	            elif ch == "\\":
702	                escaped = True
703	            elif ch == '"':
704	                in_str = False
705	            continue
706	        if ch == '"':
707	            in_str = True
708	        elif ch == open_ch:
709	            depth += 1
710	        elif ch == close_ch:
711	            depth -= 1
712	            if depth == 0:
713	                return text[start:i + 1]
714	    return None

Perfect. Now let me compile my findings. I have identified several test coverage gaps related to batch-15 behaviors:

## Summary of findings:

1. **Line 350-351**: The `status == "rejected"` fallback path for detecting rate limits is not tested. This is the fallback for SDK message shapes that don't have a RateLimitEvent.

2. **Lines 373-378**: The `ResultMessage.is_error=True` path is never tested - this is the path where the CLI reports API failures by completing the stream with is_error=True instead of raising.

3. **Lines 405-407**: The `asyncio.TimeoutError` exception handler is not tested - no test case simulates an asyncio timeout on T3.

4. **Lines 502-503**: The exception handling in baggage.get_baggage() is never triggered - we don't have a test that simulates an exception from the OTel baggage call.

5. **Test coverage in CI**: The .github/workflows/test.yml only runs `tests/test_phase0/` but not `test_reasoning_client.py`, `test_observability.py`, or `test_infrastructure/`. This means the entire new batch-15 error handling code goes untested in CI.

6. **Non-deterministic test**: `test_submit_bounded_releases_slot_on_completion` has a timing-dependent loop that could fail on slow CI runners. It polls `rc._inflight` with a 2-second timeout and relies on thread callback execution within that time.

7. **Uncovered escape sequence handling**: The `_first_balanced_json` function handles escape sequences (lines 699-704), but there's no test case with escaped characters like `{\"...\"}`within JSON strings.

content.text: Confirmed - there's no test for `is_error` or `api_error_status`. Now let me compile the final audit findings.

findings.title: Missing test coverage: T3 ResultMessage.is_error=True error path
findings.problem: The error handling path for T3 SDK's ResultMessage.is_error=True (when the CLI reports API failures by completing the stream instead of raising) is never exercised in tests. This is a critical failure mode per batch-15 #1 (pass-4), yet has no test case.
findings.best_practice_violated: Complete branch coverage for failure/recovery paths; all error modes from external systems should be tested
findings.proposed_fix: Add test_t3_complete_handles_result_message_is_error that yields a fake ResultMessage with is_error=True and api_error_status=500, verifying the code correctly captures and raises ReasoningError

findings.title: Missing test coverage: T3 SDK message status=='rejected' fallback path
findings.problem: The fallback path for detecting rate limits via message.status=='rejected' (when SDK message shapes don't have RateLimitEvent) is never tested. This is a defensive path per comment #266 that handles variant SDK message shapes.
findings.best_practice_violated: Defensive coding for variant message shapes should be tested; especially for error classification that controls retry behavior
findings.proposed_fix: Add test_t3_complete_handles_status_rejected_message that yields a fake message with status='rejected', verifying it's classified as a rate-limit error and triggers backpressure

findings.title: Missing test coverage: T3 asyncio.TimeoutError and FuturesTimeout exception handler
findings.problem: The exception handler for asyncio.TimeoutError and FuturesTimeout in _t3_complete is never triggered in tests. No test simulates an asyncio timeout on the T3 async path.
findings.best_practice_violated: Timeout exception handling on async paths should be explicitly tested; especially for high-stakes T3 calls
findings.proposed_fix: Add test_t3_complete_raises_on_asyncio_timeout that mocks _run_sync to raise asyncio.TimeoutError, verifying the timeout message is formatted and wrapped correctly

findings.title: Missing test coverage: OTel baggage.get_baggage() exception handling
findings.problem: The exception handler for baggage.get_baggage() is never triggered; the try-except block silently degrades to run_id=None if baggage fails, but this branch is untested.
findings.best_practice_violated: Degradation paths should be explicitly verified; even defensive code should be exercised
findings.proposed_fix: Add test_complete_degrades_gracefully_when_baggage_fails that mocks baggage.get_baggage to raise an exception, verifying run_id remains None and the span carries the fall-back value

findings.title: Missing test coverage: _first_balanced_json escape sequence handling
findings.problem: The escape-sequence handling logic in _first_balanced_json (handling \" within JSON strings) is present but never tested. Test covers simple string with } inside quotes but not escaped quotes.
findings.best_practice_violated: Edge cases in string parsing (especially escape sequences) should have explicit test coverage
findings.proposed_fix: Add parameterized test cases to test_first_balanced_json covering strings with escaped quotes, e.g., {"a": "\"escaped\""} and nested escapes

findings.title: CI workflow does not run test_reasoning_client.py tests
findings.problem: Batch-15 added significant new error handling to reasoning_client.py (token folding, T3 rate-limit variants, cache token tracking, permission_mode dontAsk) but the CI workflow only runs tests/test_phase0/ and skips test_reasoning_client.py, test_observability.py, and test_infrastructure/. This means all the new batch-15 error-handling code goes untested in CI.
findings.best_practice_violated: Critical cross-layer infrastructure changes (LLM execution seam) should always run in CI; especially when modifying error classification and retry logic
findings.proposed_fix: Extend .github/workflows/test.yml to also run: pytest tests/test_reasoning_client.py tests/test_observability.py tests/test_infrastructure/ in addition to test_phase0/, ensuring all error paths are validated on every CI run

findings.title: Non-deterministic test with timing-dependent polling
findings.problem: test_submit_bounded_releases_slot_on_completion polls rc._inflight with a busy-loop sleep(0.01) and a 2-second deadline. This is timing-dependent and could fail on slow CI runners or under load. The test relies on the ThreadPoolExecutor callback being invoked within the deadline.
findings.best_practice_violated: Tests should not rely on timing windows or thread callback latency; use synchronization primitives instead
findings.proposed_fix: Refactor to use a threading.Event or threading.Condition instead of polling. Signal the event from the done-callback and wait() with timeout, making the test deterministic

findings.title: Missing test for cache token folding on empty/None token counts
findings.problem: The _record_usage function (batch-15 #280) folds cache_read/creation tokens into prompt total. Test test_record_usage_folds_cache_tokens_into_total covers the happy path with all fields present, but doesn't test when cache tokens are None, or when only some token fields are present.
findings.best_practice_violated: Arithmetic operations on optional fields should handle None/missing values; test coverage should include partial data scenarios
findings.proposed_fix: Add parameterized test cases covering: no cache tokens (only input/output present), None in cache fields, missing input_tokens, and zero values to verify the folding logic is robust
